topic: best airport routes for Uber — IAH vs Hobby

turns.content: bro i need to figure out my airport game. been losing money going to the wrong one at the wrong time
Airport runs can definitely be hit or miss with timing. Are you talking about choosing between IAH and Hobby, or more about when to queue up?
both honestly. like iah is further out but the fares are bigger. hobby is closer to me but the rides are shorter most of the time

That's the classic trade-off. IAH tends to have more long-distance rides since it handles most of the international and cross-country flights, but you burn time and gas getting out there. Hobby is quicker to reach but a lot of those passengers are just doing short Southwest hops to Dallas or wherever. What times are you usually trying to work the airports?
i usually try mornings like 5-8am and then again at night maybe 9pm-midnight. those are the peak flight times right

Yeah those windows make sense. Early morning is great for catching business travelers heading out, and late night gets the arrivals from delayed flights and red-eyes. For mornings, IAH might be worth the drive since you'll get more business travelers with longer rides. Late nights at Hobby could work well since it's closer and you're not burning time driving across the city.
thats what i was thinking too. like hobby late night makes sense cuz i can do a few runs quick and go home. but mornings at iah i might get one ride to the galleria or downtown thats like 40-50 bucks

Exactly. And with IAH mornings, even if you only get one or two solid rides, you're already ahead of what you'd make doing three short Hobby runs. Plus less wear on your car. Do you ever get stuck in the airport queue for a long time though?
bro thats the worst part. sometimes you sit in that lot for 45 minutes and the whole time youre making zero. wallahi i sat at iah for over an hour one time on a tuesday morning and i was sick

Yeah that dead time in the queue is a real killer. Some drivers check flight tracking apps to see when a bunch of flights are landing before they head to the lot. That way you're not just sitting there hoping. Have you tried anything like that?
nah i havent but thats actually smart. what apps do people use for that

FlightAware and Flightradar24 are the big ones. You can look at arrivals for IAH or Hobby in real time and see when clusters of flights are landing. If you see five flights touching down within 20 minutes, that's when you want to be in the queue. Saves you from sitting there burning time on a dead stretch.
downloading flightaware rn bro. appreciate that. game changer if it works. you think weekends different too or same strategy

Weekends shift a bit. You get fewer business travelers and more families and vacationers, especially at Hobby since Southwest runs a lot of leisure routes. Hobby can actually be better on weekends because the turnaround is faster — families going short distances, quick trips. IAH weekends are more hit or miss depending on international flight schedules.
that makes sense. ight bro let me get back to it thanks for the tips wallahi im about to be strategic with this
Hope it helps you cut down on that wait time. Good luck out there tonight.
